summary: Iteration 64: Tested Explore page modal ownership detection and contextual actions. All features working correctly. Backend: GET /api/records/check-ownership returns {in_collection: bool, record_id: str|null}. GET /api/discogs/release/{id} returns catno and color_variant fields. Frontend: Modal shows variant pills (year, label, catno, format, country, color_variant), contextual buttons switch based on ownership - non-owner sees Add to Collection + Add to Wantlist, owner sees Log a Spin + List for Sale + Offer to Trade. View on Discogs link present when discogs_id available.

issues: Console warning: button cannot contain nested button (add-wantlist button inside album card button) - not blocking but should be fixed for HTML validity

action_items: Fix nested button HTML warning in ExplorePage.js - the add-wantlist-tc button is nested inside the album card button (line 265-270). Consider using a div with onClick instead of a button, or restructure to avoid nesting.
critical_code_review_comments: HTML validation: <button> inside <button> in ExplorePage.js lines 262-270 - the 'add to wantlist' button is nested inside the album card button. This causes console warnings and is invalid HTML.
Dialog accessibility warnings in console - DialogContent missing DialogTitle/Description for screen readers. Consider adding VisuallyHidden component.
seed_data_creation: Created test user testexplore@test.com for testing. Test records added during flow testing were cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 64 verified the Explore page modal ownership detection feature. Key endpoints: GET /api/records/check-ownership (params: discogs_id or artist+title, returns in_collection bool and record_id), GET /api/discogs/release/{id} (returns catno and color_variant fields). Modal shows variant details pills and contextual action buttons that switch based on ownership. Test user: testexplore@test.com / testpass123. Minor issue: nested button warning in ExplorePage.js. All APIs are real (no mocking).
